Learn Coding
← Home
About this platform

I learn by building the thing that teaches me.

This is a custom learning ecosystem built by Trevor Brown — an investigative journalist turned web developer. Every lesson, tool, game, and AI tutor here was designed and coded from scratch, because the deepest way to learn something is to build the platform that teaches it.

The Story

I spent 15+ years as an investigative journalist. Then I taught myself web development — React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind — and discovered that building things scratched the same itch as breaking a story: find the truth in complex systems and present it clearly.

Now I'm going deep on D3.js, Python/Django, and SQL for a potential role at Tenga, a chain of broadcast TV networks. Election dashboards, weather maps, census overlays, breaking-news graphics — the kind of work where data visualization meets editorial judgment.

I don't watch tutorials. I don't follow along with textbooks. I build the thing that teaches me, and in doing so, I learn it more deeply than any course could offer.

The Philosophy

Every lesson follows a single rule: mental models first, syntax second. I don't memorize API signatures. I learn the why behind every function so I can derive the how on the fly.

Each concept is taught through analogies to tools I already know. D3 scales are translators between data and pixels. Django's ORM is Prisma with a different accent. SQL window functions are spreadsheet formulas that can see neighboring rows.

I don't want to memorize syntax — I want to understand why things work so I can build anything.

What's Here

3
Active tracks
36
Lessons
10
Games
6
AI tools

Learn D3.js

12 lessons, 10 games, live sandbox, code snippets, use cases, portfolio projects, and a workstation with real data journalism.

Learn Python + Django

5 Python lessons taught through JS analogies, then 7 Django lessons building full-stack apps from scratch.

Learn SQL + PostgreSQL

12 lessons from SELECT to window functions. Taught through Prisma ORM analogies for immediate comprehension.

Four more tracks are planned: JavaScript/TypeScript, React/Next.js, CSS/Design Systems, and AI/LLMs.

Learning Tools

Every platform shares a common set of tools, all built to match how I actually learn.

AI Tutor

Context-aware Claude chatbot on every page. Knows the lesson, the concepts, and your progress.

Explain It Back

Feynman technique. The AI asks you to explain concepts, then pushes deeper on weak spots.

Audio Mode

Listen to lessons passively. Section-by-section playback for second-screen learning.

Skill Test

AI-generated adaptive assessment. 10 questions, difficulty scaling, score history.

The Scroll

Replace doomscrolling with learning. Interview Qs, hot takes, tips, and bugs from the dev timeline.

The Pod

AI-generated podcast episodes that break down each lesson into conversational audio you can learn from on the go.

Tech Stack

Deliberately minimal. No framework overhead — just fast, vanilla JavaScript served by Vite. The platforms are about learning, not showcasing framework mastery.

Vite Vanilla JS D3.js v7 Claude API Web Speech API localStorage SVG + Canvas TopoJSON

Ethics

Visualization isn't decoration — it's editorial. Every pixel is a decision about what to show, what to hide, and what to emphasize. As a journalist, that responsibility shapes every chart, map, and dashboard I build. The full ethics page covers how journalistic principles map to visualization choices.

Built With Claude Code

This entire ecosystem was built in collaboration with Claude Code. Custom AI skills for D3 tutoring, data journalism ethics, JavaScript patterns, React, CSS design systems, and AI/LLM development guide every page and feature.

Trevor Brown · 2026 — Back to home
